description_long: This is a rustic log cabin with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in wooden shingles, featuring a prominent stone chimney on one end. The lower half of the structure is constructed from horizontally stacked logs, while the upper gable section is clad in vertical wooden planks. There are a few small, shuttered windows and a single wooden door, both showing signs of age and weathering. Moss and leaf litter are visible around the base, suggesting the cabin is situated in a natural or wooded environment. The stone chimney is robust and textured, extending above the roofline. The overall appearance is that of a traditional, hand-built cabin, likely intended for residential or shelter use.
description: A weathered log cabin with a steep gable roof, stone chimney, and wooden plank upper facade, featuring small shuttered windows and a single wooden door.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from the gable end, showing the vertical wooden planks above the log walls and a shuttered window below.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the full length of the wooden shingle roof, the main entrance door, and the stone chimney at the far end.
description_view_2: The opposite gable end is dominated by the large stone chimney and features two small shuttered windows in the upper section.
description_view_3: The rear side displays the continuous shingle roof, a single shuttered window, and the log construction with some moss at the base.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with wooden shingles and a prominent stone chimney
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical arrangement with small, rectangular shuttered windows on the gable ends and one on the side; no large or grouped windows visible
attribute_door_placement: single wooden door located on the long side of the cabin, offset towards the left when facing the chimney end
